sentence_reviews.text: Limited, noisy, heuristic, or indirect labels are combined with unlabeled data.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.6 'Learning with Less Data', including transfer, multitask, one-shot, zero-shot, and active learning (lines 512-516), plus Chapter 19.8 'Unsupervised Learning' (lines 523-532).
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA supports less-data learning and unsupervised learning, but the inspected evidence does not directly cover weak supervision or noisy/heuristic labels.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Semi-supervised & weak supervision is to connect Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning to downstream capabilities including Medical imaging & clinical decision support, Computer vision, perception & scene understanding.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples' (lines 488-523), Chapter 25 'Computer Vision' (lines 657-692), and Chapter 26.4 'Robotic Perception' (lines 693-702).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support learning and computer vision/perception, but not the exact semi/weak-supervision graph role or medical-imaging downstream claim.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Medical imaging & clinical decision support
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected source does not support this specific downstream medical-imaging claim.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Computer vision, perception & scene understanding
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 25 'Computer Vision' (lines 657-692); SimCLR arXiv abstract reports semi-supervised ImageNet performance from learned representations (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected evidence supports vision applications and semi-supervised visual representation learning; the substrate wording is atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples' (lines 488-523) and Chapter 27 'Philosophy, Ethics, and Safety of AI' (lines 731-763).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports the broad learning context, but not the exact limitation sentence.

sentence_reviews.text: Semi-supervised & weak supervision follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.2 'Supervised Learning' (line 493) and Chapter 19.6 'Learning with Less Data' (lines 512-516).
sentence_reviews.notes: Semi-supervised/weak supervision uses supervised label information, but the source does not state this combined category exactly.

sentence_reviews.text: Semi-supervised & weak supervision follows the learning setup expressed by Unsupervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.6 'Learning with Less Data' (lines 512-516) and Chapter 19.8 'Unsupervised Learning' (lines 523-532).
sentence_reviews.notes: Use of unlabeled data makes the unsupervised-learning connection defensible, but the exact rationale is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Semi-supervised & weak supervision uses Labels, targets, rewards & preferences as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.2 'Supervised Learning' (line 493) and Chapter 19.6 'Learning with Less Data' (lines 512-516).
sentence_reviews.notes: Limited labels are supported by the concept, but rewards/preferences are broader than the inspected evidence.

edge_reviews.notes: The relation to supervised learning is defensible because labels remain part of the setup; the combined weak-supervision category is not directly sourced.
The relation to unsupervised learning is defensible because unlabeled data is part of semi-supervised setups.
Label/signal use is supported, but the broad supervision-signals node includes rewards and preferences not established by the inspected evidence.
